title: Vented dental appliance for bruxism relief
abstract: A vented dental appliance (10) for relief of bruxism includes an intraoral retainer (12) such as a flexible plate (18) positioned between the teeth and the cheeks of the user. An elongated vent member (20) is slidably positioned through one of a. plurality of apertures (19) in the intraoral retainer (12) and includes at least a first fluid conduit (21) connecting the intraoral space and the extraoral space. The intraoral portion of the elongated vent member (20) features multiple intraoral vent openings (25) positioned along thereof and configured to prevent internal blockage by a tongue or other soft tissue inside the mouth. The extraoral portion is open and configured to vent the intraoral space to atmosphere, thereby relieving the intraoral suction and allowing for jaw muscles to relax. In other embodiments, the intraoral retainer (12) may be a dental guard or another more advanced dental appliance.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A vented dental appliance 10 for relief of bruxism, wherein the improvement is characterized by the dental appliance 10 comprising: an elongated vent member 20 comprising a first fluid conduit 21 between one or more intraoral vent openings 25 positioned on an end of and along an intraoral portion thereof and an extraoral opening of an extraoral portion thereof, and an intraoral retainer 12 configured for slidably retaining the elongated vent member 20 positioned across thereof to provide fluid communication between an intraoral space and an extraoral space, thereby relieving intraoral suction, wherein the elongated vent member 20 is prevented from sliding across the intraoral retainer 12 unless subjected to a pull force at or above a predetermined pull threshold. 2. The dental appliance as in claim 1 , wherein the predetermined pull threshold is at least 0.25 lbs. 3. The dental appliance as in claim 1 , wherein the predetermined pull threshold is at least 0.5 lbs. 4. The dental appliance as in claim 1 , wherein the predetermined pull threshold is at least 0.75 lbs. 5. The dental appliance as in claim 1 , wherein the elongated vent member 20 protrudes through a first aperture 19 in the intraoral retainer 12 forming a friction fit therewith, the intraoral retainer 12 is configured to not occlude the fluid conduit 21 at the location of the first aperture 19. 6. The dental appliance as in claim 5, wherein the intraoral retainer 12 comprises a second alternative aperture 19 configured for retaining the elongated vent member 20, the second aperture is located adjacent to the first aperture, thereby using either the first aperture or the second aperture for retention of the elongated vent 

 member providing for position adjustment of the eiongated vent member across the intraorai retainer 12. 7. The dental appliance as in claim 6, wherein the intraoral retainer 12 comprises a plurality of further apertures 19 each configured for individually retaining the elongated vent member 20, thereby providing a plurality of alternate positions of the elongated vent member across the intraoral retainer 12. 8. The dental appliance as in claim 1 , wherein the elongated vent member 20 comprises a second fluid conduit 22 positioned across the intraoral retainer 12 and configured to provide further fluid communication between the intraoral space and the extraoral space. 9. The dental appliance as in claim 8, wherein the first fluid conduit 21 of the elongated vent member 20 is positioned on a first side 14 of the intraoral retainer 12, while the second fluid conduit 22 of the elongated vent member 20 is positioned on a second side 16 thereof. 10. A vented dental appliance 10 for relief of bruxism, wherein the improvement is characterized by the dental appliance 10 comprising: an elongated vent member 20 comprising a first fluid conduit 21 between one or more intraoral vent openings 25 positioned at an end of and along an intraoral portion thereof and an extraoral opening of an extraoral portion thereof, and an intraoral retainer 12 configured for retaining the elongated vent member 20 positioned across thereof through a first aperture 19 to provide fluid communication between an intraoral space and an extraoral space, thereby relieving intraoral suction, wherein the intraoral retainer 12 further comprising a second alternative aperture 19 configured for retaining the elongated vent member 20, the second aperture is located adjacent to the first aperture, thereby using either the first aperture or the second aperture for retention of the elongated vent member 20 facilitating position adjustment of the elongated vent member 20 across the intraoral retainer 12. 11. The vented dental appliance as in claim 10, wherein the intraoral retainer 12 contains a plurality of apertures 19 configured for allowing alternative positioning of the elongated vent member 20 across thereof. 12. The vented dental appliance as in claim 11 , wherein the plurality of apertures 19 are arranged to facilitate adjustment of horizontal positioning and vertical positioning of the elongated vent member 20 across thereof. 13. The vented dental appliance as in claim 12, wherein the plurality of apertures 19 are arranged in at least two parallel rows of apertures. 14. The vented appliance as in claim 13, wherein each row of apertures 19 contains at least 3 apertures therein. 15. A vented dental appliance 10 for relief of bruxism, wherein the improvement is characterized by the dental appliance comprising: an elongated vent member 20 comprising a first fluid conduit 21 between multiple intraoral vent openings 25 positioned along an intraoral portion thereof and an extraoral opening 28 of an extraoral portion thereof, and an intraoral retainer 12 configured for removably retaining the elongated vent member 20 positioned across thereof to provide fluid communication between an intraoral space and an extraoral space, thereby relieving intraoral suction. 16. The vented dental appliance as in claim 15, wherein the elongated vent member 20 further comprises at least one grommet 70 positioned over the first fluid conduit 21 and configured to define the position thereof across the intraoral retainer 12. 17. The vented dental appliance as in claim 16, wherein the at least one grommet 70 is slidable along the first fluid conduit 21 upon applying a force exceeding a predetermined pull threshold. 18. The vented dental appliance as in claim 16, wherein the at least one grommet 70 is permanently attached to the first fluid conduit 21 and is located on the extraoral portion thereof. 19. The vented dental appliance as in claim 16, wherein the at least one grommet 70 has at least one leading edge on at least one side thereof, thereby facilitating sliding of the first fluid conduit across the aperture of the intraoral retainer. 20. The vented dental appliance as in claim 16, wherein the at least grommet 70 has an elongated cross-section.
